summary: Iteration 4 testing: PWA Push Notifications and GPS-based Crew Proximity Matching features. All 10 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI confirmed working for both crew and contractor dashboards.
critical_code_review_comments: push_routes.py and push_utils.py are correctly placed under /app/backend/routes/ and /app/backend/utils/ (not in /app/backend/ root as mentioned in files_of_reference - minor doc discrepancy only, code works fine)

test_results.POST /api/push/subscribe (auth required): PASS - returns 401 without auth, 200 with auth
test_results.POST /api/push/test: PASS - returns delivered count (0 expected in test env, no active browser subscription)
test_results.GET /api/jobs?lat=33.749&lng=-84.388&rad: PASS - Atlanta jobs returned with distance_miles field
test_results.GET /api/jobs (no lat/lng): PASS - no distance_miles field on any job
test_results.App Settings - Web Push section: PASS - 'Enable Push' button present, permission denied message shown

mocked_apis: Web Push: VAPID keys real but browser subscription not available in test env, delivered=0 is expected and confirmed
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both new features (PWA Push Notifications and GPS proximity matching) are fully functional. Backend routes at /api/push/* all working. GPS distance_miles field correctly added to job responses when lat/lng params provided. All auth flows stable. App Settings page shows Web Push section with 'Permission denied' state since test browser blocks push API.
